title: Lesson 59
paragraph: This lesson focuses on practical B1 vocabulary for everyday communication.

words.meaning: to wish to have or do something
words.example: I want a quiet place to study.

words.meaning: to look at something for a period of time
words.example: We watch a movie every Friday.

words.meaning: to be the best or first in a game or contest
words.example: Our team can win if we practice.

words.meaning: to make words with a pen, pencil, or keyboard
words.example: Please write your email address clearly.

words.meaning: to agree to take or receive something
words.example: She will accept the job offer.
The train will arrive at noon.

words.meaning: to use something and return it later
to damage something or make it stop working
words.example: Do not break the glass cup.

words.meaning: to make something by putting parts together
words.example: They will build a new library.

words.meaning: to decide that a plan or event will not happen
words.example: We may cancel the picnic if it rains.
